artPrompt: A ramshackle goblin coin-mint built into a town-square alcove, glowing forges spitting flame-orange sparks as a freshly struck coin flips spinning into the air, foreshortened bright toward the viewer, while a sleeping goblin slumps over the press and another winks from the shadows. Overturned ballot boxes and a bonfire flicker beyond in night-black smoke, gleeful shoddy industry, no central character. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Two-Bit Goblin Mint
text:
When Two-Bit Goblin Mint enters the battlefield, the goblins strike a fresh coin. Flip it. If you win the flip, you get a Treasure token. If you lose the flip, a goblin pockets it and you get nothing but a knowing wink.
{T}: Add {R}.
{T}, flip a coin: If you win the flip, add {R}{R} instead. If you lose, the coin rolls under the fridge.
flavorText: Quality control at the mint is one goblin who is asleep.
